documents.title: Natives architecture
documents.summary: This document details the three-layer architecture of the Natives stack, covering the TypeScript API wrapper, the runtime addon loading and validation layer, and the underlying Rust N-API implementation layer.

documents.title: Session
documents.summary: This document defines the canonical JSONL format, taxonomy, and structural semantics used for representing, persisting, and reconstructing coding-agent sessions, including versioning and migration rules.

documents.title: Tui
documents.summary: This document details the current Terminal User Interface (TUI) contract between the rendering engine and integration layers for implementing custom extensions and tools, covering component contracts, rendering constraints, input handling, and lifecycle management.

documents.title: Sdk
documents.summary: This document details the structure, installation, and usage of the `@oh-my-pi/pi-coding-agent` SDK for in-process integration, covering session creation, session management persistence, model wiring, event subscription, and tool integration.

documents.title: Rpc
documents.summary: This document serves as a reference detailing the Remote Procedure Call (RPC) protocol implemented over newline-delimited JSON (JSONL) on stdio for controlling a coding agent, covering command/response schemas, event streams, and concurrency behavior.

documents.title: Models
documents.summary: This document details the structure, configuration, and runtime resolution process for models and providers using the `models.yml` configuration file, covering authentication, discovery, and context promotion.

documents.title: Config usage
documents.summary: This document details the configuration resolution process used by the coding-agent, covering root scanning order, precedence rules across different configuration sources, and how the final resolved configuration is consumed by various subsystems like settings, skills, and tools.

documents.title: Environment variables
documents.summary: This document serves as a runtime reference detailing all environment variables that configure providers, authentication, and specific runtime behavior for the coding agent, including precedence rules for loading these variables.

documents.title: Extension loading
documents.summary: This document explains the process by which the coding agent discovers, resolves, imports, and executes extension modules written in TypeScript or JavaScript at startup.

documents.title: Theme
documents.summary: This document details the structure, loading process, validation rules, and runtime behavior of the coding agent's theming system, covering required color tokens, variable resolution, and persistence of theme settings.

documents.title: Extensions
documents.summary: This document serves as the primary guide for authoring runtime extensions for the coding agent, detailing the structure of an extension module, the extension lifecycle model, and the various API surfaces available for registration, actions, context access, and event handling.

documents.title: Hooks
documents.summary: This document details the internal implementation, key files, event surfaces, and execution semantics of the system's hook subsystem, noting its current status as an API surface layered on top of the extension runner.

documents.title: Mcp protocol transports
documents.summary: This document details the internal implementation of the MCP JSON-RPC messaging protocol, focusing on how protocol logic is separated from transport-specific concerns like stdio and HTTP/SSE.

documents.title: Mcp runtime lifecycle
documents.summary: This document details the complete lifecycle of Managed Code Process (MCP) servers within the coding-agent runtime, covering discovery, connection, tool exposure, live session refreshes, and teardown procedures.

documents.title: Mcp server tool authoring
documents.summary: This document details the architecture and process by which MCP server definitions are loaded, validated, connected, and exposed as callable tools within the coding-agent environment.

documents.title: Gemini manifest extensions
documents.summary: This document details the discovery mechanism for Gemini-style manifest extensions located in `gemini-extension.json`, explaining how the coding agent scans user and project directories and materializes them into capability items without performing runtime module loading.

documents.title: Custom tools
documents.summary: This document explains how to define, implement, and integrate custom tools (model-callable functions) into an execution pipeline using a specific factory pattern in TypeScript/JavaScript modules.

documents.title: Task agent discovery
documents.summary: This document explains the runtime process for how the task subsystem discovers, merges, resolves, and enforces availability constraints on agent definitions from various sources.

documents.title: Bash tool runtime
documents.summary: This document details the runtime path and execution flow for the 'bash' tool in the agent, covering input normalization, security interception, PTY vs. non-PTY selection, execution engines, and output handling, including truncation and artifact spilling.

documents.title: Notebook tool runtime
documents.summary: This document details the internal architecture of the `notebook` tool, clarifying that it functions solely as a JSON editor for .ipynb files and is distinct from the kernel-backed execution path handled by the separate `python` tool.

documents.title: Python repl
documents.summary: This document details the architecture and behavior of the Python execution tool, covering the use of a Jupyter Kernel Gateway, session management, environment resolution, and output rendering for code execution.

documents.title: Resolve tool runtime
documents.summary: This document explains the internal mechanics of how preview/apply workflows are managed using the hidden `resolve` tool and how custom tools can participate by pushing pending actions via `pushPendingAction` to support explicit user confirmation.

documents.title: Session tree plan
documents.summary: This document details the current in-memory session tree architecture, explaining its append-only log persistence contrasted with its runtime tree model defined by parent-child relationships, leaf movement semantics, and the distinct navigation flows for internal tree traversal (/tree) versus creating new session branches (/branch).

documents.title: Session switching and recent listing
documents.summary: This document details the internal implementation mechanics for how the coding agent discovers, lists, selects, and switches between active and recent runtime sessions, including logic for startup resume arguments and in-session selection pickers.

documents.title: Session operations export share fork resume
documents.summary: This document details the implementation and behavior of various session management operations, including exporting, dumping, sharing, forking, and resuming sessions, primarily through interactive commands and CLI flags.

documents.title: Compaction
documents.summary: This document details the compaction and branch summary mechanisms used to maintain context in long sessions by rewriting old history into summaries, outlining implementation details, session entry models, pipeline triggers, and cut-point logic.

documents.title: Non compaction retry policy
documents.summary: This document details the non-compaction auto-retry mechanism used by AgentSession for handling API errors, outlining the classification logic, retry lifecycle, backoff semantics, and configuration controls.

documents.title: Memory
documents.summary: This document explains the Autonomous Memory feature, which enables an agent to automatically extract durable knowledge from past sessions and consolidate it into a project-scoped memory store injected into new sessions.

documents.title: Blob artifact architecture
documents.summary: This document explains the dual storage architecture used for payloads, separating large binary data into content-addressed blobs and session-specific tool/subagent outputs into artifacts, detailing their naming conventions, persistence flow, and URL resolution mechanisms.

documents.title: Fs scan cache architecture
documents.summary: This document details the architecture, contracts, and usage guidelines for the shared filesystem scan cache implemented in Rust, which caches directory scan results to avoid repeated filesystem walks for discovery and search operations.

documents.title: Natives addon loader runtime
documents.summary: This document details the runtime logic within `@oh-my-pi/pi-natives` responsible for loading, validating, and managing native addon binaries, including platform detection and embedded payload extraction.

documents.title: Natives binding contract
documents.summary: This document specifies the TypeScript-side contract definition, wrapper behavior, and public export surface for the native bindings used between application callers and the N-API addon.

documents.title: Natives build release debugging
documents.summary: This runbook details the process for building, releasing, and debugging native Node.js addons using the `@oh-my-pi/pi-natives` pipeline, covering build scripts, artifact discovery, naming conventions, and runtime loading logic.

documents.title: Natives media system utils
documents.summary: This document provides a deep-dive technical overview of the implementation details, data flow boundaries, and lifecycle management for native system utilities related to image processing, HTML conversion, clipboard interaction, and work profiling.

documents.title: Natives rust task cancellation
documents.summary: This document explains the implementation of native Rust task scheduling and the cooperative cancellation mechanism (`CancelToken`) used to propagate signals and timeouts from JavaScript execution contexts into Rust code.

documents.title: Natives shell pty process
documents.summary: This document details the internal architecture and implementation layers for the shell, pseudo-terminal (PTY), process-tree management, and key parsing primitives within the `@oh-my-pi/pi-natives` library.

documents.title: Natives text search pipeline
documents.summary: This document details the mapping and implementation architecture between the TypeScript text/search wrappers (grep, glob, text, highlight) and their underlying Rust N-API exports, including flow control and error handling for each subsystem.

documents.title: Tui runtime internals
documents.summary: This document details the internal architecture and runtime flow of a Text User Interface (TUI) system, mapping terminal input through processing layers in `packages/tui` to state-driven UI updates managed by `packages/coding-agent` controllers.

documents.title: Skills
documents.summary: This document details the structure, discovery pipeline, runtime usage, and protocol behaviors for file-backed capability packs known as Skills within the codebase.

documents.title: Slash command internals
documents.summary: This document details the internal mechanisms for discovering, deduplicating, resolving conflicts, and utilizing slash commands within the coding agent system, covering file scanning, provider precedence, and prompt pipeline integration.

documents.title: Handoff generation pipeline
documents.summary: This document details the implementation pipeline for the `/handoff` command in the coding-agent, covering the trigger path, prompt generation, context capture, session switching, and data injection into the new session.

documents.title: Provider streaming internals
documents.summary: This document details the internal mechanisms used by `@oh-my-pi/pi-ai` to normalize, throttle, and propagate token and tool streaming events from various AI providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google) into a unified contract for upstream agents and sessions.

documents.title: Plugin manager installer plumbing
documents.summary: This document details the architecture and lifecycle of the plugin management system, focusing on how plugins are installed, managed on disk, and loaded at runtime via the PluginManager.

documents.title: Secrets
documents.summary: This document explains how to enable and configure secret obfuscation, a feature designed to prevent sensitive values like API keys from being sent to LLM providers by replacing them with deterministic placeholders.

documents.title: Porting from pi mono
documents.summary: This document provides a detailed, step-by-step checklist for merging and porting changes from an upstream repository named 'pi-mono' into the current repository, focusing on adapting code to Bun-specific APIs, package scopes, and style conventions.

documents.title: Porting to natives
documents.summary: This document provides a practical guide and checklist for porting performance-critical paths from JavaScript to native Rust modules using N-API within the project structure.

documents.title: Rulebook matching pipeline
documents.summary: This document details the process by which the coding-agent discovers configuration rules from various sources, normalizes them into a canonical shape, resolves precedence, and splits them into rulebook and time-travel stream interruption (TTSR) sets.

documents.title: Ttsr injection lifecycle
documents.summary: This document details the runtime lifecycle for Time Traveling Stream Rules (TTSR), covering rule discovery, stream monitoring, trigger decision process, retry injection logic based on context mode, repeat policy enforcement, and event emission surfaces.

documents.title: Tree
documents.summary: This document serves as a reference guide for the `/tree` command, which opens an interactive Session Tree navigator allowing users to jump to any entry within the current session file for navigation, editing, or branching.
